title: Magic as Merchandise
description: First time the party enters a shop selling magic scrolls/spells. Short beat, no plot advance.

steps.text: The shop sells spells. Not scrolls — spells. Each one is labeled, priced, and wrapped.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Fire in a bottle. Twenty gold.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Is that more or less than real fire?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Considerably less, and the shopkeeper knows it.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: In the temple we prayed for these. For weeks. And here a farmer's son hands one over for coin.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Progress is usually a little insulting to whoever came before.

steps.text: The party bought one spell. They did not, as a group, discuss which.
